hydrogen is a liquid below what temperature hydrogen is a liquid below what temperature hydrogen is a highly versatile and unique element that exists in various states of matter under different conditions. at standard atmospheric pressure, hydrogen is a gas, but as the temperature decreases, it undergoes a phase transition. specifically, hydrogen becomes a liquid below a temperature of 20.28 kelvin (-252.87 degrees celsius or -422.97 degrees fahrenheit). this extremely low temperature is required to liquefy hydrogen, making it one of the coldest liquids known. the process of liquefying hydrogen is complex and requires advanced cryogenic technology, but it has numerous applications in industries such as space exploration, energy storage, and scientific research
